functions.name: aoc_wim.aoc2017.q15_slow.duel
documentation: - The modulo (2147483647) is a mersenne prime M31: 2 ** 31 - 1
- The factor for generator A (16807) is 7 ** 5 (a primitive root module M31)
- The factor for generator B (48271) is prime
I don't know how to exploit these facts at all, but they're actually well-known
These specific factors specify a Lehmer or Park-Miller PRNG implementation:
The factors are chosen by hand because they produce a full period generating
function with good randomness. The original 1988 paper describing this is here:
Maybe some obscure bit hack here can help?

methods.name: aoc_wim.search.AStar.heuristic
documentation:
estimate of distance (cost) to get from state0 to state1
note: in order for the heuristic to be admissible, this must
not overestimate the actual distance (i.e. be optimistic)

methods.name: aoc_wim.search.AStar.cost
documentation: actual delta to get from current state to an adjacent state

methods.name: aoc_wim.search.AStar.adjacent
documentation: other states reachable from given state

functions.name: aoc_wim.stuff.factorise
documentation:
returns the sorted prime factors of natural number n

```pycon
>>> factorise(12)
[2, 2, 3]
>>> factorise(4998)
[2, 3, 7, 7, 17]
>>> factorise(4999)
[4999]
```

functions.name: aoc_wim.aoc2015.q11.req1
documentation:
Passwords must include one increasing straight of at least three letters, 
like abc, bcd, cde, and so on, up to xyz. They cannot skip letters; abd 
doesn't count.

functions.name: aoc_wim.aoc2015.q11.req2
documentation:
Passwords may not contain the letters i, o, or l, as these letters can be 
mistaken for other characters and are therefore confusing.

functions.name: aoc_wim.aoc2015.q11.req3
documentation:
Passwords must contain at least two different, non-overlapping pairs of 
letters, like aa, bb, or zz.

functions.name: aoc_wim.aoc2022.q15.line_intersection
documentation: the point we're looking for should be just beyond the perimeter of 4 different scanners
the perimeter in manhattan distance is a diamond shape ◇
when 4 edges intersect it will look like a rotated #
we can find intersections of edges like / and \ use as candidates for the search
when such a point is out of range of *all* the sensors, we have found the solution
